test.observation: Vitest exited 1 with exactly 9 known pre-existing failures confined to src/db/__tests__/schema.test.ts (8) and src/app/api/health-data/__tests__/route.test.ts (1), while src/db/__tests__/audit-immutability.test.ts remained skipped; no new failing test files were introduced.

typecheck.observation: tsc --noEmit completed successfully with exit code 0.

lint.observation: next lint completed with no ESLint warnings or errors.

blockingIssues.description: useNightView types hypnogram as an array, but the night endpoint returns an object `{ stages, total_duration_hr } | null`, creating a contract mismatch for consumers.
useNightView types baselines as `Record<string, BaselinePayload>`, but `/api/views/night` returns `{ avg, stddev, upper, lower }` baseline objects.
useRecoveryView defines daily entries with an embedded `date` field, but `/api/views/recovery` returns `daily` keyed by date with `{ metrics }` payloads and no nested date property.
useTrendView types per-metric `baseline` as `BaselinePayload | null`, but `/api/views/trend` returns `{ avg, stddev, upper, lower } | null`.
InsightCard dismiss behavior is not wired to `useDismissInsight` as required by the feature contract.

Shared time-axis alignment is broken: MetricStripContainer provides numeric xDomain values while MetricStrip keeps ISO string x-values and switches to numeric time scale, causing domain/value type mismatch.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a hooks-only execution path that does not require component-centric steps (component TDD and visual browser checks) when a feature scope is data hooks/types only.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: react-query-hooks review and transcript indicate the skill's component-focused procedure did not fit a hooks-only deliverable.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Make agent-browser verification mandatory only for features with explicit visual/UI interaction requirements; keep it optional for non-UI code paths.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: dashboard-shared-components review observed stated mandatory browser verification in the skill, while worker execution and handoff relied on automated tests only.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Refine strict tests-first wording to allow implementation/testing in small pairs or batches, while still requiring equivalent coverage and accurate deviation reporting.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: dashboard-shared-components review captured repeated friction with strict TDD ordering versus practical multi-component delivery flow.
